items.title: Bolsonaro's conviction brings vindication for some Brazilians who lost loved ones to COVID-19
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>With Brazil's former President Jair Bolsonaro preparing to serve a 27-year sentence for attempting a coup, many families who lost loved ones to COVID-19 say the conviction offers a measure of vindication even though it is unrelated to his handling of the pandemic.(AP video by Lucas Dumphreys and Maycron Abade)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Prince William and Princess of Wales meet Paddington
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The royal couple bent down to shake a paw of the "Paddington: The Musical" star, as they met him and his show's composer Tom Fletcher at the Royal Variety Performance in London, Wednesday.

#paddington #royals #princewilliam

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:

items.title: Trump learns about the arrest of Brazil's Jair Bolsonaro from media
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>President Donald Trump was asked outside the White House on Saturday about the arrest of former Brazilian president Jair Bolsonaro, but said that was the first he was hearing of it.

items.title: Bolsonaro arrested over alleged plot to escape and avoid 27-year prison term
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Brazil's federal police on Saturday arrested former president Jair Bolsonaro over suspicion he was plotting to escape and avoid starting a 27-year prison sentence for leading a coup attempt. (AP video by Lucas Dumphreys).

items.title: UN climate talks end with deal for more money to countries hit by climate change
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>United Nations climate talks in Brazil reached a subdued agreement Saturday to deliver more money to countries hit hardest by climate change to help them adapt to extreme weather’s wrath. (AP Video produced by Aya Diab).

items.title: As researchers hunt for treatments for kids with autoimmune diseases, this summer camp brings joy
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>It may sound surprising but kids can get diseases like lupus and arthritis, too, when their immune system mistakenly attacks their own body. Treatment is challenging for growing bodies, and researchers are hunting for better options. But doctors in New York helped some affected kids experience of their first sleepaway camp — despite strict medications and nervous parents.

#shorts #summercamp #autoimmune #sleepawaycamp</article>

items.title: Communities of slave descendants in Brazil demand recognition of territories
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Forty minutes from this year's U.N. climate talks, communities of slave descendants are pushing the Brazilian government to give them titles to lands that they say they have been on for generations. (AP Video by Teresa de Miguel. Produced by Julián Trejo Bax).

#brazil
